# Mental Health Specialists in Ibero-America

> Interactive dashboard on mental health and neurology specialists per 100,000 inhabitants in Ibero-America (2000–2026).

## Author
Juan Moisés de la Serna Tuya (UNIR)
ORCID: 0000-0002-8401-8018
Email: jmsernatuya@gmail.com

## Description
Open dataset tracking the density of mental health specialists (psychiatrists, psychologists, neurologists, social workers, nurses) per 100,000 inhabitants across 20+ Ibero-American countries from 2000 to 2026. Enables cross-country comparisons of mental healthcare access and identifies regions with critical shortages.

## Coverage
- Countries: Spain, Portugal, Mexico, Argentina, Brazil, Colombia, Chile, Peru, Venezuela, Ecuador, Bolivia, Paraguay, Uruguay, Cuba, Dominican Republic, Costa Rica, Panama, Honduras, Guatemala, El Salvador, Nicaragua
- Years: 2000–2026
- Variables: psychiatrists, psychologists, neurologists, social workers, nurses, total specialists per 100k

## Dataset DOI
https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.18984813

## Dashboard URL
https://juanmoisesd.github.io/mental-health-specialists-iberoamerica/

Serna Tuya, J.M. (2025). Mental Health Specialists in Ibero-America: Open Dataset (2000–2026). Zenodo. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.18984813
